result:
Everything builds and verifies cleanly. Here's the summary:

## What was wrong

The GSC export showed a company mid-pivot: Lumina used to be "InboxZap" (email cleanup), and 100% of the site's existing organic traffic (18 clicks, ~13k impressions across the blog) was still tied to the old brand and email-cleanup topics — zero visibility for the actual current product. Live-testing the app (the given port `58586` wasn't actually serving this repo — it returned an unrelated "Baxter" site and hung on every sub-path, so I ran the app locally instead) confirmed why:

- Homepage `<title>`/description/OG still said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes" while the content was 100% Lumina — Google-vs-content mismatch.
- Nav + sitemap linked to `/features` and `/pricing` — both 404, no such routes existed.
- Blog index fetched a nonexistent `/api/posts` (client-side, so crawlers saw nothing) and all 12 previously-indexed post URLs 404'd — no `[slug]` route existed at all.
- The OG image referenced in metadata also 404'd.

## What I fixed

- **Metadata** (`app/layout.tsx`): title/description/OG now describe the real product.
- **Brand bridge** (`app/page.tsx`): added a line noting Lumina is the new name for InboxZap, to protect the existing brand-query traffic instead of confusing those searchers outright.
- **Built `/features` and `/pricing`** as real, indexable pages so nav and sitemap links resolve.
- **Fixed the blog**: server-rendered index (`app/blog/page.tsx`), a proper `[slug]` dynamic route with per-post metadata, and 4 new posts targeting the product's actual keyword space (AI meeting notes, action items, meeting summaries) — the topics GSC shows zero current visibility for.
- **301-redirected the 12 dead `/blog/post-N` URLs** to `/blog` (via `next.config.js`) instead of leaving them 404, since they're still indexed with real impression volume.
- **Fixed `sitemap.ts`** to list real pages/posts instead of the stale TODO.
- **Added a working dynamic OG image** (`app/opengraph-image.tsx`) to replace the broken static reference.

Verified with `next build` — all 13 routes compile and statically generate cleanly, including the 4 blog posts and sitemap.

**Next step worth doing outside this session**: get the real dev server running on the port you intended (or tell me the right one) so I can re-verify against it directly, and confirm whether `usebaxter.com` showing up on `58586` is a stale process worth killing.
